source_quote: One of the batteries may show "reverse voltage" while others are well below "critical". Measure each of the 8 cells individually with a DMM; use a 90 W charger and attempt to bring cells back to 3.4 V per cell.

evidence.source_quote_substring: One of the batteries may show "reverse voltage"
evidence.reasoning: F2 is the battery fuse/protection component feeding BAT1FUSED rail; a cell going into reverse voltage or deep discharge causes the protection to open the circuit.
The protective fuse/path opens under reverse-voltage or critically low cell conditions.

evidence.source_quote_substring: Measure each of the 8 cells individually with a DMM
evidence.reasoning: With F2 open, the BAT1FUSED rail downstream is dead.

source_quote: A prior report was incorrectly attributed to a detached port but turned out to be a faulty cable; a later confirmed case involved the port actually detaching after upgrading to a newer motherboard + sound board. Visual inspection and gentle tug test on the connector shell.

evidence.source_quote_substring: the port actually detaching after upgrading to a newer motherboard + sound board
evidence.reasoning: J4 is a connector in the graph representing the micro-HDMI port; mechanical detachment of the port creates an open circuit on the HDMI output path.
Physical detachment of the connector pads from the PCB creates an open circuit.

source_quote: Attaching a camera, mouse, or NFC reader produces nothing — no power, no data. Try all ports; a partially functional port (enumeration errors only) can help isolate whether the fault is in the VBUS switch or the data path.

evidence.source_quote_substring: a partially functional port (enumeration errors only) can help isolate whether the fault is in the VBUS switch or the data path
evidence.reasoning: U24 is the USB_PWR source (VBUS switch) in the graph. The source explicitly calls out the VBUS switch as the suspected component.
The USB port delivers no power and no data — the VBUS switch (U24) is completely dead.

evidence.source_quote_substring: the fault is in the VBUS switch
evidence.reasoning: USB_PWR rail (sourced by U24) would be dead when the VBUS switch fails.

source_quote: A small hardware modification on the R-2 production motherboard improves charging/battery balancing, together with an LPC firmware update and a keyboard firmware update.

evidence.source_quote_substring: A small hardware modification on the R-2 production motherboard improves charging/battery balancing
evidence.reasoning: U2 is the INTVCC/VOUT source in the graph, associated with charging circuitry. The hardware modification addresses charging/balancing degradation.
The charging circuit is not fully dead but underperforming — cells diverge in voltage — modelled as regulating_low.
Charging voltage is present but insufficient to fully balance cells, estimated at 88% of nominal.
